page: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable performance instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the t3.xlarge operates in lower performance tiers under sustained workloads. In CPU-heavy tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, the instance scores in the bottom 10% to 25% categories. Memory bandwidth tests, including large block read and write operations, also fall into the bottom 25% tier, with read speeds of 23.93 GB/sec and write speeds of 20.41 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET benchmarks show weak performance, reflecting the burstable CPU allocation model which is designed for variable workloads rather than continuous high-throughput execution.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3.xlarge offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that experience occasional spikes. Because CPU resources are burstable, it is qualitatively cost-effective for development environments, code repositories, low-traffic web servers, and small database instances. However, the lack of local storage and the lower benchmark scores make it less suitable for sustained high-performance computing, large-scale data processing, or real-time LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. Operating at 2.5 GHz, it features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores and is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the t3.xlarge in the lower tiers, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and memory bandwidth tests consistently scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. This performance profile reflects its burstable CPU allocation, which is designed to handle temporary demand spikes rather than sustained heavy compute loads. Consequently, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for low-intensity workloads such as development environments, code repositories, and low-traffic web serving, where continuous high-performance resources are not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for budget-friendly workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor for variable workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly burstable instance offering balanced memory and compute resources for variable, low-intensity development and testing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB DDR4 RAM
Burstable CPU allocation model designed for variable, non-sustained workloads
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for development environments, code repositories, and low-traffic web serving
